NKJV.1: A man who isolates himself seeks his own desire; He rages against all wise judgment.
NKJV.2: A fool has no delight in understanding, But in expressing his own heart.
NKJV.3: When the wicked comes, contempt comes also; And with dishonor comes reproach.
NKJV.4: The words of a man’s mouth are deep waters; The wellspring of wisdom is a flowing brook.
NKJV.5: It is not good to show partiality to the wicked, Or to overthrow the righteous in judgment.
NKJV.6: A fool’s lips enter into contention, And his mouth calls for blows.
NKJV.7: A fool’s mouth is his destruction, And his lips are the snare of his soul.
NKJV.8: The words of a talebearer are like tasty trifles, And they go down into the inmost body.
NKJV.9: He who is slothful in his work Is a brother to him who is a great destroyer.
NKJV.10: The name of the LORD is a strong tower; The righteous run to it and are safe.
NKJV.11: The rich man’s wealth is his strong city, And like a high wall in his own esteem.
NKJV.12: Before destruction the heart of a man is haughty, And before honor is humility.
NKJV.13: He who answers a matter before he hears it, It is folly and shame to him.
NKJV.14: The spirit of a man will sustain him in sickness, But who can bear a broken spirit?
NKJV.15: The heart of the prudent acquires knowledge, And the ear of the wise seeks knowledge.
NKJV.16: A man’s gift makes room for him, And brings him before great men.
NKJV.17: The first one to plead his cause seems right, Until his neighbor comes and examines him.
NKJV.18: Casting lots causes contentions to cease, And keeps the mighty apart.
NKJV.19: A brother offended is harder to win than a strong city, And contentions are like the bars of a castle.
NKJV.20: A man’s stomach shall be satisfied from the fruit of his mouth; From the produce of his lips he shall be filled.
NKJV.21: Death and life are in the power of the tongue, And those who love it will eat its fruit.
NKJV.22: He who finds a wife finds a good thing, And obtains favor from the LORD.
NKJV.23: The poor man uses entreaties, But the rich answers roughly.
NKJV.24: A man who has friends must himself be friendly, But there is a friend who sticks closer than a brother.

KJV.1: Through desire<S>8378</S> a man, having separated<S>6504</S> himself, seeketh<S>1245</S> and intermeddleth<S>1566</S> with all wisdom<S>8454</S>. <sup>Through: or, He that separateth himself seeketh according to his desire, and intermeddleth in every business</sup>
KJV.2: A fool<S>3684</S> hath no delight<S>2654</S> in understanding<S>8394</S>, but that his heart<S>3820</S> may discover<S>1540</S> itself.

KJV.5: It is not good<S>2896</S> to accept<S>5375</S> the person<S>6440</S> of the wicked<S>7563</S>, to overthrow<S>5186</S> the righteous<S>6662</S> in judgment<S>4941</S>.

KJV.7: A fool's<S>3684</S> mouth<S>6310</S> is his destruction<S>4288</S>, and his lips<S>8193</S> are the snare<S>4170</S> of his soul<S>5315</S>.
KJV.8: The words<S>1697</S> of a talebearer<S>5372</S> are as wounds<S>3859</S>, and they go down<S>3381</S> into the innermost parts<S>2315</S> of the belly<S>990</S>. <sup>talebearer: or, whisperer</sup> <sup>as wounds: or, like as when men are wounded</sup> <sup>most: Heb. chambers</sup>
KJV.9: He also that is slothful<S>7503</S> in his work<S>4399</S> is brother<S>251</S> to him that is a great<S>1167</S> waster<S>7843</S>.
KJV.10: The name<S>8034</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> is a strong<S>5797</S> tower<S>4026</S>: the righteous<S>6662</S> runneth<S>7323</S> into it, and is safe<S>7682</S>. <sup>safe: Heb. set aloft</sup>
KJV.11: The rich man's<S>6223</S> wealth<S>1952</S> is his strong<S>5797</S> city<S>7151</S>, and as an high<S>7682</S> wall<S>2346</S> in his own conceit<S>4906</S>.
KJV.12: Before<S>6440</S> destruction<S>7667</S> the heart<S>3820</S> of man<S>376</S> is haughty<S>1361</S>, and before<S>6440</S> honour<S>3519</S> is humility<S>6038</S>.
KJV.13: He that answereth<S>7725</S> a matter<S>1697</S> before he heareth<S>8085</S> it, it is folly<S>200</S> and shame<S>3639</S> unto him. <sup>answereth: Heb. returneth a word</sup>
KJV.14: The spirit<S>7307</S> of a man<S>376</S> will sustain<S>3557</S> his infirmity<S>4245</S>; but a wounded<S>5218</S> spirit<S>7307</S> who can bear<S>5375</S>?
KJV.15: The heart<S>3820</S> of the prudent<S>995</S> getteth<S>7069</S> knowledge<S>1847</S>; and the ear<S>241</S> of the wise<S>2450</S> seeketh<S>1245</S> knowledge<S>1847</S>.
KJV.16: A man's<S>120</S> gift<S>4976</S> maketh room<S>7337</S> for him, and bringeth<S>5148</S> him before<S>6440</S> great men<S>1419</S>.
KJV.17: He that is first<S>7223</S> in his own cause<S>7379</S> seemeth just<S>6662</S>; but his neighbour<S>7453</S> cometh<S>935</S> <S>935</S> and searcheth<S>2713</S> him.
KJV.18: The lot<S>1486</S> causeth contentions<S>4079</S> to cease<S>7673</S>, and parteth<S>6504</S> between the mighty<S>6099</S>.

KJV.20: A man's<S>376</S> belly<S>990</S> shall be satisfied<S>7646</S> with the fruit<S>6529</S> of his mouth<S>6310</S>; and with the increase<S>8393</S> of his lips<S>8193</S> shall he be filled<S>7646</S>.
KJV.21: Death<S>4194</S> and life<S>2416</S> are in the power<S>3027</S> of the tongue<S>3956</S>: and they that love<S>157</S> it shall eat<S>398</S> the fruit<S>6529</S> thereof.
KJV.22: Whoso findeth<S>4672</S> a wife<S>802</S> findeth<S>4672</S> a good<S>2896</S> thing, and obtaineth<S>6329</S> favour<S>7522</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S>.
KJV.23: The poor<S>7326</S> useth<S>1696</S> intreaties<S>8469</S>; but the rich<S>6223</S> answereth<S>6030</S> roughly<S>5794</S>.
KJV.24: A man<S>376</S> that hath friends<S>7453</S> must shew himself friendly<S>7489</S>: and there is<S>3426</S> a friend<S>157</S> that sticketh closer<S>1695</S> than a brother<S>251</S>.

ESV.1: Whoever isolates himself seeks his own desire; he breaks out against all sound judgment.
ESV.2: A fool takes no pleasure in understanding, but only in expressing his opinion.
ESV.3: When wickedness comes, contempt comes also, and with dishonor comes disgrace.
ESV.4: The words of a man's mouth are deep waters; the fountain of wisdom is a bubbling brook.
ESV.5: It is not good to be partial to  the wicked or to deprive the righteous of justice.
ESV.6: A fool's lips walk into a fight, and his mouth invites a beating.
ESV.7: A fool's mouth is his ruin, and his lips are a snare to his soul.
ESV.8: The words of a whisperer are like delicious morsels; they go down into the inner parts of the body.
ESV.9: Whoever is slack in his work is a brother to him who destroys.
ESV.10: The name of the LORD is a strong tower; the righteous man runs into it and is safe.
ESV.11: A rich man's wealth is his strong city, and like a high wall in his imagination.
ESV.12: Before destruction a man's heart is haughty, but humility comes before honor.
ESV.13: If one gives an answer before he hears, it is his folly and shame.
ESV.14: A man's spirit will endure sickness, but a crushed spirit who can bear?
ESV.15: An intelligent heart acquires knowledge, and the ear of the wise seeks knowledge.
ESV.16: A man's gift makes room for him and brings him before the great.
ESV.17: The one who states his case first seems right, until the other comes and examines him.
ESV.18: The lot puts an end to quarrels and decides between powerful contenders.
ESV.19: A brother offended is more unyielding than a strong city, and quarreling is like the bars of a castle.
ESV.20: From the fruit of a man's mouth his stomach is satisfied; he is satisfied by the yield of his lips.
ESV.21: Death and life are in the power of the tongue, and those who love it will eat its fruits.
ESV.22: He who finds a wife finds a good thing and obtains favor from the LORD.
ESV.23: The poor use entreaties, but the rich answer roughly.
ESV.24: A man of many companions may come to ruin, but there is a friend who sticks closer than a brother.

NASB.1: He who separates himself seeks [his own] desire, He quarrels against all sound wisdom.
NASB.2: A fool does not delight in understanding, But only in revealing his own mind.
NASB.3: When a wicked man comes, contempt also comes, And with dishonor [comes] scorn.
NASB.4: The words of a man's mouth are deep waters; The fountain of wisdom is a bubbling brook.
NASB.5: To show partiality to the wicked is not good, [Nor] to thrust aside the righteous in judgment.
NASB.6: A fool's lips bring strife, And his mouth calls for blows.
NASB.7: A fool's mouth is his ruin, And his lips are the snare of his soul.
NASB.8: The words of a whisperer are like dainty morsels, And they go down into the innermost parts of the body.
NASB.9: He also who is slack in his work Is brother to him who destroys.
NASB.10: The name of the LORD is a strong tower; The righteous runs into it and is safe.
NASB.11: A rich man's wealth is his strong city, And like a high wall in his own imagination.
NASB.12: Before destruction the heart of man is haughty, But humility [goes] before honor.
NASB.13: He who gives an answer before he hears, It is folly and shame to him.
NASB.14: The spirit of a man can endure his sickness, But [as for] a broken spirit who can bear it?
NASB.15: The mind of the prudent acquires knowledge, And the ear of the wise seeks knowledge.
NASB.16: A man's gift makes room for him And brings him before great men.
NASB.17: The first to plead his case [seems] right, [Until] another comes and examines him.
NASB.18: The [cast] lot puts an end to strife And decides between the mighty ones.
NASB.19: A brother offended [is harder to be won] than a strong city, And contentions are like the bars of a citadel.
NASB.20: With the fruit of a man's mouth his stomach will be satisfied; He will be satisfied [with] the product of his lips.
NASB.21: Death and life are in the power of the tongue, And those who love it will eat its fruit.
NASB.22: He who finds a wife finds a good thing And obtains favor from the LORD.
NASB.23: The poor man utters supplications, But the rich man answers roughly.
NASB.24: A man of [too many] friends [comes] to ruin, But there is a friend who sticks closer than a brother.

NLT.1:
Unfriendly people care only about themselves;
they lash out at common sense.
NLT.2:
Fools have no interest in understanding;
they only want to air their own opinions.
NLT.3:
Doing wrong leads to disgrace,
and scandalous behavior brings contempt.
NLT.4:
Wise words are like deep waters;
wisdom flows from the wise like a bubbling brook.
NLT.5:
It is not right to acquit the guilty
or deny justice to the innocent.
NLT.6:
Fools’ words get them into constant quarrels;
they are asking for a beating.
NLT.7:
The mouths of fools are their ruin;
they trap themselves with their lips.
NLT.8:
Rumors are dainty morsels
that sink deep into one’s heart.
NLT.9:
A lazy person is as bad as
someone who destroys things.
NLT.10:
The name of the LORD is a strong fortress;
the godly run to him and are safe.
NLT.11:
The rich think of their wealth as a strong defense;
they imagine it to be a high wall of safety.
NLT.12:
Haughtiness goes before destruction;
humility precedes honor.
NLT.13:
Spouting off before listening to the facts
is both shameful and foolish.
NLT.14:
The human spirit can endure a sick body,
but who can bear a crushed spirit?
NLT.15:
Intelligent people are always ready to learn.
Their ears are open for knowledge.
NLT.16:
Giving a gift can open doors;
it gives access to important people!
NLT.17:
The first to speak in court sounds right —
until the cross-examination begins.
NLT.18:
Flipping a coin can end arguments;
it settles disputes between powerful opponents.
NLT.19:
An offended friend is harder to win back than a fortified city.
Arguments separate friends like a gate locked with bars.
NLT.20:
Wise words satisfy like a good meal;
the right words bring satisfaction.
NLT.21:
The tongue can bring death or life;
those who love to talk will reap the consequences.
NLT.22:
The man who finds a wife finds a treasure,
and he receives favor from the LORD.
NLT.23:
The poor plead for mercy;
the rich answer with insults.
NLT.24:
There are “friends” who destroy each other,
but a real friend sticks closer than a brother.

WEB.1: An unfriendly man pursues selfishness, and defies all sound judgment.
WEB.2: A fool has no delight in understanding, but only in revealing his own opinion.
WEB.3: When wickedness comes, contempt also comes, and with shame comes disgrace.
WEB.4: The words of a man’s mouth are like deep waters. The fountain of wisdom is like a flowing brook.
WEB.5: To be partial to the faces of the wicked is not good, nor to deprive the innocent of justice.
WEB.6: A fool’s lips come into strife, and his mouth invites beatings.
WEB.7: A fool’s mouth is his destruction, and his lips are a snare to his soul.
WEB.8: The words of a gossip are like dainty morsels: they go down into a person’s innermost parts.
WEB.9: One who is slack in his work is brother to him who is a master of destruction.
WEB.10: Yahweh’s name is a strong tower: the righteous run to him, and are safe.
WEB.11: The rich man’s wealth is his strong city, like an unscalable wall in his own imagination.
WEB.12: Before destruction the heart of man is proud, but before honor is humility.
WEB.13: He who gives answer before he hears, that is folly and shame to him.
WEB.14: A man’s spirit will sustain him in sickness, but a crushed spirit, who can bear?
WEB.15: The heart of the discerning gets knowledge. The ear of the wise seeks knowledge.
WEB.16: A man’s gift makes room for him, and brings him before great men.
WEB.17: He who pleads his cause first seems right; until another comes and questions him.
WEB.18: The lot settles disputes, and keeps strong ones apart.
WEB.19: A brother offended is more difficult than a fortified city; and disputes are like the bars of a fortress.
WEB.20: A man’s stomach is filled with the fruit of his mouth. With the harvest of his lips he is satisfied.
WEB.21: Death and life are in the power of the tongue; those who love it will eat its fruit.
WEB.22: Whoever finds a wife finds a good thing, and obtains favor of Yahweh.
WEB.23: The poor plead for mercy, but the rich answer harshly.
WEB.24: A man of many companions may be ruined, but there is a friend who sticks closer than a brother.

AMP.1:
He who [willfully] separates himself [from God and man] seeks his own desire,
He quarrels against all sound wisdom.
AMP.2:
A [closed-minded] fool does not delight in understanding,
But only in revealing his personal opinions [unwittingly displaying his self-indulgence and his stupidity].
AMP.3:
When the wicked man comes [to the depth of evil], contempt [of all that is pure and good] also comes,
And with inner baseness (dishonor) comes outer shame (scorn).
AMP.4:
The words of a man's mouth are like deep waters [copious and difficult to fathom];
The fountain of [mature, godly] wisdom is like a bubbling stream [sparkling, fresh, pure, and life-giving].
AMP.5:
To show respect to the wicked person is not good,
Nor to push aside and deprive the righteous of justice.
AMP.6:
A fool's lips bring contention and strife,
And his mouth invites a beating.
AMP.7:
A fool's mouth is his ruin,
And his lips are the snare of his soul.
AMP.8:
The words of a whisperer (gossip) are like dainty morsels [to be greedily eaten];
They go down into the innermost chambers of the body [to be remembered and mused upon].
AMP.9:
He who is careless in his work
Is a brother to him who destroys.
AMP.10:
The name of the LORD is a strong tower;
The righteous runs to it and is safe and set on high [far above evil].
AMP.11:
The rich man's wealth is his strong city,
And like a high wall [of protection] in his own imagination and conceit.
AMP.12:
Before disaster the heart of a man is haughty and filled with self-importance,
But humility comes before honor.
AMP.13:
He who answers before he hears [the facts]—
It is folly and shame to him.
AMP.14:
The spirit of a man sustains him in sickness,
But as for a broken spirit, who can bear it?
AMP.15:
The mind of the prudent [always] acquires knowledge,
And the ear of the wise [always] seeks knowledge.
AMP.16:
A man's gift [given in love or courtesy] makes room for him
And brings him before great men.
AMP.17:
The first one to plead his case seems right,
Until another comes and cross-examines him.
AMP.18:
To cast lots puts an end to quarrels
And decides between powerful contenders.
AMP.19:
A brother offended is harder to win over than a fortified city,
And contentions [separating families] are like the bars of a castle.
AMP.20:
A man's stomach will be satisfied with the fruit of his mouth;
He will be satisfied with the consequence of his words.
AMP.21:
Death and life are in the power of the tongue,
And those who love it and indulge it will eat its fruit and bear the consequences of their words.
AMP.22:
He who finds a [ true and faithful] wife finds a good thing
And obtains favor and approval from the LORD.
AMP.23:
The poor man pleads,
But the rich man answers roughly.
AMP.24:
The man of too many friends [chosen indiscriminately] will be broken in pieces and come to ruin,
But there is a [true, loving] friend who [is reliable and] sticks closer than a brother.

MSG.1:
Loners who care only for themselves
spit on the common good.
MSG.2:
Fools care nothing for thoughtful discourse;
all they do is run off at the mouth.
MSG.3:
When wickedness arrives, shame's not far behind;
contempt for life is contemptible.
MSG.4:
Many words rush along like rivers in flood,
but deep wisdom flows up from artesian springs.
MSG.5:
It's not right to go easy on the guilty,
or come down hard on the innocent.
MSG.6:
The words of a fool start fights;
do him a favor and gag him.
MSG.7:
Fools are undone by their big mouths;
their souls are crushed by their words.
MSG.8:
Listening to gossip is like eating cheap candy;
do you really want junk like that in your belly?
MSG.9:
Slack habits and sloppy work
are as bad as vandalism.
MSG.10:
God's name is a place of protection—
good people can run there and be safe.
MSG.11:
The rich think their wealth protects them;
they imagine themselves safe behind it.
MSG.12:
Pride first, then the crash,
but humility is precursor to honor.
MSG.13:
Answering before listening
is both stupid and rude.
MSG.14:
A healthy spirit conquers adversity,
but what can you do when the spirit is crushed?
MSG.15:
Wise men and women are always learning,
always listening for fresh insights.
MSG.16:
A gift gets attention;
it buys the attention of eminent people.
MSG.17:
The first speech in a court case is always convincing—
until the cross-examination starts!
MSG.18:
You may have to draw straws
when faced with a tough decision.
MSG.19:
Do a favor and win a friend forever;
nothing can untie that bond.
MSG.20:
Words satisfy the mind as much as fruit does the stomach;
good talk is as gratifying as a good harvest.
MSG.21:
Words kill, words give life;
they're either poison or fruit—you choose.
MSG.22:
Find a good spouse, you find a good life—
and even more: the favor of God!
MSG.23:
The poor speak in soft supplications;
the rich bark out answers.
MSG.24:
Friends come and friends go,
but a true friend sticks by you like family.

NIV.1:
An unfriendly man pursues selfish ends;
he defies all sound judgment.
NIV.2:
A fool finds no pleasure in understanding
but delights in airing his own opinions.
NIV.3:
When wickedness comes, so does contempt,
and with shame comes disgrace.
NIV.4:
The words of a man’s mouth are deep waters,
but the fountain of wisdom is a bubbling brook.
NIV.5:
It is not good to be partial to the wicked
or to deprive the innocent of justice.
NIV.6:
A fool’s lips bring him strife,
and his mouth invites a beating.
NIV.7:
A fool’s mouth is his undoing,
and his lips are a snare to his soul.
NIV.8:
The words of a gossip are like choice morsels;
they go down to a man’s inmost parts.
NIV.9:
One who is slack in his work
is brother to one who destroys.
NIV.10:
The name of the Lord is a strong tower;
the righteous run to it and are safe.
NIV.11:
The wealth of the rich is their fortified city;
they imagine it an unscalable wall.
NIV.12:
Before his downfall a man’s heart is proud,
but humility comes before honor.
NIV.13:
He who answers before listening—
that is his folly and his shame.
NIV.14:
A man’s spirit sustains him in sickness,
but a crushed spirit who can bear?
NIV.15:
The heart of the discerning acquires knowledge;
the ears of the wise seek it out.
NIV.16:
A gift opens the way for the giver
and ushers him into the presence of the great.
NIV.17:
The first to present his case seems right,
till another comes forward and questions him.
NIV.18:
Casting the lot settles disputes
and keeps strong opponents apart.
NIV.19:
An offended brother is more unyielding than a fortified city,
and disputes are like the barred gates of a citadel.
NIV.20:
From the fruit of his mouth a man’s stomach is filled;
with the harvest from his lips he is satisfied.
NIV.21:
The tongue has the power of life and death,
and those who love it will eat its fruit.
NIV.22:
He who finds a wife finds what is good
and receives favor from the Lord.
NIV.23:
A poor man pleads for mercy,
but a rich man answers harshly.
NIV.24:
A man of many companions may come to ruin,
but there is a friend who sticks closer than a brother.

NRSVCE.1:
The one who lives alone is self-indulgent,
showing contempt for all who have sound judgment.
NRSVCE.2:
A fool takes no pleasure in understanding,
but only in expressing personal opinion.
NRSVCE.3:
When wickedness comes, contempt comes also;
and with dishonor comes disgrace.
NRSVCE.4:
The words of the mouth are deep waters;
the fountain of wisdom is a gushing stream.
NRSVCE.5:
It is not right to be partial to the guilty,
or to subvert the innocent in judgment.
NRSVCE.6:
A fool’s lips bring strife,
and a fool’s mouth invites a flogging.
NRSVCE.7:
The mouths of fools are their ruin,
and their lips a snare to themselves.
NRSVCE.8:
The words of a whisperer are like delicious morsels;
they go down into the inner parts of the body.
NRSVCE.9:
One who is slack in work
is close kin to a vandal.
NRSVCE.10:
The name of the LORD is a strong tower;
the righteous run into it and are safe.
NRSVCE.11:
The wealth of the rich is their strong city;
in their imagination it is like a high wall.
NRSVCE.12:
Before destruction one’s heart is haughty,
but humility goes before honor.
NRSVCE.13:
If one gives answer before hearing,
it is folly and shame.
NRSVCE.14:
The human spirit will endure sickness;
but a broken spirit — who can bear?
NRSVCE.15:
An intelligent mind acquires knowledge,
and the ear of the wise seeks knowledge.
NRSVCE.16:
A gift opens doors;
it gives access to the great.
NRSVCE.17:
The one who first states a case seems right,
until the other comes and cross-examines.
NRSVCE.18:
Casting the lot puts an end to disputes
and decides between powerful contenders.
NRSVCE.19:
An ally offended is stronger than a city;
such quarreling is like the bars of a castle.
NRSVCE.20:
From the fruit of the mouth one’s stomach is satisfied;
the yield of the lips brings satisfaction.
NRSVCE.21:
Death and life are in the power of the tongue,
and those who love it will eat its fruits.
NRSVCE.22:
He who finds a wife finds a good thing,
and obtains favor from the LORD.
NRSVCE.23:
The poor use entreaties,
but the rich answer roughly.
NRSVCE.24:
Some friends play at friendship
but a true friend sticks closer than one’s nearest kin.

NET.1: One who has isolated himself seeks his own desires; he rejects all sound judgment.
NET.2: A fool takes no pleasure in understanding but only in disclosing what is on his mind.
NET.3: When a wicked person arrives, contempt shows up with him, and with shame comes a reproach.
NET.4: The words of a person’s mouth are like deep waters, and the fountain of wisdom is like a flowing brook.
NET.5: It is terrible to show partiality to the wicked, by depriving a righteous man of justice.
NET.6: The lips of a fool enter into strife, and his mouth invites a flogging.
NET.7: The mouth of a fool is his ruin, and his lips are a snare for his life.
NET.8: The words of a gossip are like choice morsels; they go down into the person’s innermost being.
NET.9: The one who is slack in his work is a brother to one who destroys.
NET.10: The name of the LORD is like a strong tower; the righteous person runs to it and is set safely on high.
NET.11: The wealth of a rich person is like a strong city, and it is like a high wall in his imagination.
NET.12: Before destruction the heart of a person is proud, but humility comes before honor.
NET.13: The one who gives an answer before he listens — that is his folly and his shame.
NET.14: A person’s spirit sustains him through sickness — but who can bear a crushed spirit?
NET.15: The discerning person acquires knowledge, and the wise person seeks knowledge.
NET.16: A person’s gift makes room for him, and leads him before important people.
NET.17: The first to state his case seems right, until his opponent begins to cross-examine him.
NET.18: A toss of a coin ends disputes, and settles the issue between strong opponents.
NET.19: A relative offended is harder to reach than a strong city, and disputes are like the barred gates of a fortified citadel.
NET.20: From the fruit of a person’s mouth his stomach is satisfied, with the product of his lips is he satisfied.
NET.21: Death and life are in the power of the tongue, and those who love its use will eat its fruit.
NET.22: The one who finds a wife finds what is enjoyable, and receives a pleasurable gift from the LORD.
NET.23: A poor person makes supplications, but a rich man answers harshly.
NET.24: A person who has friends may be harmed by them, but there is a friend who sticks closer than a brother.
